metadata.comments: Solution template for Speculus Threat Intelligence

metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

connectorUiConfig.title: Speculus Threat Intelligence
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Ingest STIX 2.1 threat intelligence indicators from the Speculus TAXII 2.1 server using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). The Speculus feed provides IP indicators enriched with risk scoring, named attribution, scanner/Tor/proxy classification, and geo/network context.

sampleQueries.query: Speculus_Indicators_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where RiskScore >= 75 | project TimeGenerated, IndicatorValue, RiskScore, Activity, Attribution, Labels
Speculus_Indicators_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where isnotempty(Attribution) | summarize Indicators = dcount(IndicatorValue) by Attribution | order by Indicators desc

dataTypes.name: Speculus_Indicators_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: Speculus_Indicators_CL | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

customs.name: Speculus API key
customs.description: A Speculus API key with access to the IOC feed. The key is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Contact Speculus to obtain one.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Speculus Threat Intelligence
instructionSteps.description:
Provide your Speculus TAXII server details and API key, then click Connect.

>Unless Speculus has provided different values, use the default TAXII base URL `https://feed.speculus.co/api1` and collection ID `f3a1c2d4-5b6e-4a7f-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d` (the `speculus-ioc-feed` collection).

>Note: polling the objects endpoint is metered against your Speculus API quota, the same as the REST API.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | where type == "indicator" | extend TimeGenerated = now(), StixId = tostring(id), Name = tostring(name), Description = tostring(description), Pattern = tostring(pattern), IndicatorValue = extract(@"value\s*=\s*'([^']+)'", 1, tostring(pattern)), IndicatorTypes = strcat_array(indicator_types, ","), Labels = strcat_array(labels, ","), Confidence = toint(confidence), RiskScore = toint(x_speculus_score), Risk = tostring(x_speculus_risk), Activity = tostring(x_speculus_activity), Attribution = tostring(x_speculus_attribution), Created = todatetime(created), Modified = todatetime(modified), ValidFrom = todatetime(valid_from), FirstSeen = todatetime(x_speculus_first_seen), LastSeen = todatetime(x_speculus_last_seen), IsScanner = tobool(x_speculus_is_scanner), ScannerName = tostring(x_speculus_scanner_name), IsTorNode = tobool(x_speculus_is_tor_node), IsBlacklisted = tobool(x_speculus_is_blacklisted), IsDatacenter = tobool(x_speculus_is_datacenter), IsVpnProxy = tobool(x_speculus_vpn_proxy), CloudProvider = x_speculus_cloud_provider, ResidentialProxy = x_speculus_residential_proxy, Isp = tostring(x_speculus_identity.isp), Org = tostring(x_speculus_identity.org), Asn = tostring(x_speculus_identity.asn), ConnectionType = tostring(x_speculus_identity.connection_type), City = tostring(x_speculus_location.city), Country = tostring(x_speculus_location.country), CountryCode = tostring(x_speculus_location.country_code) | project TimeGenerated, StixId, Name, Description, Pattern, IndicatorValue, IndicatorTypes, Labels, Confidence, RiskScore, Risk, Activity, Attribution, Created, Modified, ValidFrom, FirstSeen, LastSeen, IsScanner, ScannerName, IsTorNode, IsBlacklisted, IsDatacenter, IsVpnProxy, CloudProvider, ResidentialProxy, Isp, Org, Asn, ConnectionType, City, Country, CountryCode

connectorUiConfig.title: Speculus Threat Intelligence
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Ingest STIX 2.1 threat intelligence indicators from the Speculus TAXII 2.1 server using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). The Speculus feed provides IP indicators enriched with risk scoring, named attribution, scanner/Tor/proxy classification, and geo/network context.

sampleQueries.query: Speculus_Indicators_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where RiskScore >= 75 | project TimeGenerated, IndicatorValue, RiskScore, Activity, Attribution, Labels
Speculus_Indicators_CL | where TimeGenerated >= ago(7d) | where isnotempty(Attribution) | summarize Indicators = dcount(IndicatorValue) by Attribution | order by Indicators desc

dataTypes.name: Speculus_Indicators_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery: Speculus_Indicators_CL | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated) | where isnotempty(Time)

customs.name: Speculus API key
customs.description: A Speculus API key with access to the IOC feed. The key is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Contact Speculus to obtain one.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Speculus Threat Intelligence
instructionSteps.description:
Provide your Speculus TAXII server details and API key, then click Connect.

>Unless Speculus has provided different values, use the default TAXII base URL `https://feed.speculus.co/api1` and collection ID `f3a1c2d4-5b6e-4a7f-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d` (the `speculus-ioc-feed` collection).

>Note: polling the objects endpoint is metered against your Speculus API quota, the same as the REST API.

properties.description: Speculus - IP Indicator Match CommonSecurityLog_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.1.0

Matches network events in CommonSecurityLog against high-risk Speculus IP indicators (risk score >= 75)
received in the last 14 days. These IPs are associated with malicious activity such as command and control,
botnets, ransomware infrastructure, or brute-force campaigns.
properties.displayName: Speculus - Network traffic to or from high-risk IP indicator
properties.query:
let indicators = Speculus_Indicators_CL
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where isnotempty(IndicatorValue)
    | where RiskScore >= 75
    | summarize arg_max(Modified, *) by IndicatorValue
    | project
        IndicatorValue,
        RiskScore,
        SpeculusActivity = Activity,
        Attribution,
        Labels,
        IndicatorDescription = Description;
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where isnotempty(SourceIP) or isnotempty(DestinationIP)
| extend MatchIP = iff(isnotempty(DestinationIP), DestinationIP, SourceIP)
| join kind=inner indicators on $left.MatchIP == $right.IndicatorValue
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    MatchIP,
    SourceIP,
    DestinationIP,
    DeviceVendor,
    DeviceProduct,
    RiskScore,
    SpeculusActivity,
    Attribution,
    Labels,
    IndicatorDescription

properties.displayName: Speculus - Network traffic to or from high-risk IP indicator

properties.description: Speculus - IP Indicator Match SigninLogs_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.1.0

Matches Microsoft Entra ID sign-in attempts against high-risk Speculus IP indicators (risk score >= 75)
received in the last 14 days. A sign-in from a known C2, botnet, or brute-force source IP may indicate
credential theft or account takeover.
properties.displayName: Speculus - Sign-in attempt from high-risk IP indicator
properties.query:
let indicators = Speculus_Indicators_CL
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where isnotempty(IndicatorValue)
    | where RiskScore >= 75
    | summarize arg_max(Modified, *) by IndicatorValue
    | project
        IndicatorValue,
        RiskScore,
        SpeculusActivity = Activity,
        Attribution,
        Labels,
        IndicatorDescription = Description;
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where isnotempty(IPAddress)
| join kind=inner indicators on $left.IPAddress == $right.IndicatorValue
| project
    TimeGenerated,
    IPAddress,
    UserPrincipalName,
    UserDisplayName,
    AppDisplayName,
    ResultType,
    ResultDescription,
    RiskScore,
    SpeculusActivity,
    Attribution,
    Labels,
    IndicatorDescription

properties.displayName: Speculus - Sign-in attempt from high-risk IP indicator

properties.description: Speculus - Feed Outage Detection_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.1.0

Detects when no Speculus threat intelligence indicators have been received for more than 24 hours.
The Speculus indexer refreshes continuously, so a silent day usually means an expired API key,
a connector misconfiguration, or a feed-side outage rather than an empty feed.
properties.query:
Speculus_Indicators_CL
| summarize LastReceived = max(TimeGenerated)
| where isnull(LastReceived) or LastReceived < ago(1d)
| extend
    HoursSinceLastIndicator = iff(isnull(LastReceived), long(null), datetime_diff('hour', now(), LastReceived)),
    Status = iff(isnull(LastReceived), 'No data ever received in query period', 'Feed silent for more than 24 hours')

metadata.description: Required. Your Speculus API key, sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
Base URL of the Speculus REST API. Change only if Speculus provides a different host.
Microsoft Sentinel workspace name (scopes the hidden-SentinelWorkspaceId tag).

body.message:
@{concat('**Speculus Threat Intelligence** — ', items('For_Each_IP_Entity')?['Address'], '

', '| Field | Value |
|---|---|
', '| Risk score | ', string(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_score']), ' (', outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_risk'], ') |
', '| Activity | ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_activity'], 'n/a'), ' |
', '| Attribution | ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_attribution'], 'n/a'), ' |
', '| Labels | ', join(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['labels'], ', '), ' |
', '| Scanner | ', string(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_is_scanner']), ' | 
', '| Tor exit node | ', string(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_is_tor_node']), ' |
', '| Blacklisted | ', string(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_is_blacklisted']), ' |
', '| ISP / ASN | ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_identity']?['isp'], 'n/a'), ' / ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_identity']?['asn'], 'n/a'), ' |
', '| Location | ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_location']?['city'], 'n/a'), ', ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['x_speculus_location']?['country'], 'n/a'), ' |
', '| Verdict | ', coalesce(outputs('Get_Indicator_Object')?['description'], 'n/a'), ' |')}

metadata.title: Speculus Incident Enrichment - Playbook
metadata.description: Runs on a Microsoft Sentinel incident, looks up every IP entity against the Speculus REST API (single-IP lookup), and posts the full Speculus enrichment (risk score, attribution, scanner/Tor/proxy classification, geo/network context) as an incident comment.
prerequisites: Speculus API key (Bearer token, same key used for the TAXII data connector or REST API)
Microsoft Sentinel workspace with Sentinel enabled
An authorized Microsoft Sentinel API connection (created during deployment; requires one-time interactive authorization after deploy)
postDeployment: 1. Open the deployed Logic App's API connections and authorize the 'azuresentinel' connection.
3. Attach it to an automation rule (recommended: run on incident creation) or run it manually from an incident's 'Run playbook' action.
4. Confirm the Speculus API key has enough REST quota for your incident volume (this endpoint is quota-metered, unlike the TAXII data connector).

releaseNotes.title: SpeculusIncidentEnrichment_Playbook
notes: Initial on-demand incident enrichment playbook using the Speculus REST single-IP lookup endpoint.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Speculus Threat Intelligence solution ingests STIX 2.1 IP indicators from the Speculus TAXII 2.1 server into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). Indicators carry risk scoring, named attribution, scanner/Tor/proxy classification, and geo/network enrichment. The solution deploys a REST API poller connector, a custom log table, a data collection rule, analytics rules for matching indicators against network and sign-in telemetry, and an incident-enrichment playbook that looks up IP entities against the Speculus REST API on demand.

Data Connectors: 1, Analytic Rules: 3, Playbooks: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
